title: Dump 02
description: 50 questions extracted from Dump02.html. Explanations were not present in the source HTML.

questions.question: Which of the following options is not an output of Project Initiation?

options.text: Definition of Ready and Definition of Done checklists.
Delivery Practice Alignment.
Vision Document.
Detail of what will be delivered in each iteration.

questions.question: What are some of the values that guide the Scrum methodology?

options.text: Agreement, respect, collaboration.
Transparency, leadership, commitment.
Commitment, respect, focus.
Courage, leadership, focus.

questions.question: Which logs are NOT recorded by the platform by default?

options.text: All errors
General Logs created by action Log
Extensions methods call...
SOAP and REST calls

questions.question: Considering the Solution Release phase, which of the following options is true?

options.text: A rule of thumb for the duration of the Solution Release Phase is: one week of Solution Release, for two weeks of Iteration Development.
Training is the responsibility of the Tech Lead, or when unavailable, of the Delivery Specialist.
No new developments or any other major tasks should take place in this phase.
The rollout plan exclusively focuses on a step by step technical deployment script.

questions.question: What is a good reason to split a core module in two?

options.text: To separate unrelated concepts with different lifecycles
To make sure that entities are isolated from business logic
To avoid upward references
To increase development speed

questions.question: Abstraction is key to promote:

options.text: The development of logic and data around business concepts directly in an end-user module, if those concepts are only used in that module
The split of a concept into different modules to separate data, logic and presentation layers
The capacity to create/update/delete entity records directly in a end-user module
Information hiding to easy the reuse of functional and non functional concepts

questions.question: In which of the following scenarios should a custom Style Guide be implemented?

options.text: Always. Every project / application should have their own custom Style Guide
Whenever an application needs to have independent deployments and low-level of interdependence among other applications.

Whenever applications cannot benefit from any existing theme. The Style Guide should be implemented by cloning the most complex template available, to leverage the Blocks and Actions from the original template and accelerate developments.

Whenever applications cannot benefit from any existing theme. The Style Guide should be implemented by starting from the most basic template possible, since it only contains the minimum required structure to start.

questions.question: During refactor, which prioritization should be checked after End-users modules are ok?

options.text: Cycles among end users
Cycles among cores
Cycles among libraries
Upward references to cores

questions.question: Regarding Stabilization, which of these statements is correct?

options.text: It is the activity performed after a user story is considered done.
The tech lead and a subset of developers perform this activity.
This activity happens in the post-production phase.
It is where a decision is made about whether the functionality that still needs to be accepted will finally be accepted or rejected.

questions.question: What is the the goal of a correct application composition?

options.text: Minimize impacts between modules
Allow different paces in modules of the same Application
Aggregate modules of same functional area, for different sponsors
Simplify deployments

questions.question: Which of the following is an Assembling principle?

options.text: Disclose business concepts and integration needs
Isolate all business concepts
Join all integrations into a single Module
Keep concepts with different lifecycles apart

questions.question: Consider the following example of a basic External Core Service, where the Master of Customer is an external ERP

options.text: The ERP is protected from end user load since there is no synchronization process
The integration service only needs to support the retrieval of customer updates since the last sync
Consumers of the Core Service will be able to compose customer information with aggregates
The ERP API must support all customer info retrieval needs, since there is no local replica in the core service

questions.question: Why is it so important to have a multi-layer framework to support Architecture Design?

options.text: To optimize lifecycle independence.
To minimize impact of changes.
To promote a correct abstraction of reusable services.
All the options are correct.

questions.question: Consider a developer with the default Developer role (with "Change and deploy applications" permissions in Dev and QA). Can they add an application to the deployment plan from Dev to QA?

options.text: Yes, because the developer has "Change and deploy applications" permission in Dev.
No, because the developer needs at least "Open and debug applications" permission in quality assurance.
Yes, because the developer has "Change and deploy applications" permission in quality assurance.
No, because the developer cannot have "Change and deploy applications" in quality assurance.

questions.question: When are user stories detailed?

options.text: Only during the Iteration preparation phase.
User stories are detailed when inserted into the product backlog.
In Initiation and in the Iteration preparation phase.
In Initiation all User Stories are detailed because that is when the entire project planning is done.

questions.question: One of the most important tasks of the Iteration Development phase is to write and prioritize the backlog for an Iteration X. When should this task be done?

options.text: In the previous iteration (X-1).
During iteration X.
At the beginning of iteration X.
At the end of iteration X.

questions.question: What is the impact of having so many violations in this application canvas?

options.text: Unmanageable deployments in lifetime
Unbalanced proportion of core applications, but still a reasonable lifecycle independence among end user / Orchestration applications
The only way to solve all the upward references is by refactoring the modules inside each application
It is impossible to move modules between applications

questions.question: What is the first thing to ensure to correctly compose applications?

options.text: No cycles among applications
Applications must be manually positioned in a layer
Application of a certain layer should only contain modules of that layer
Modules correctly layered with no violations

questions.question: Check the following blueprint where a shared Style guide provides the menu and the Login flow in the theme. In this situation, what is the applicable scenario?

options.text: Independent App
Portal
There is no applicable scenario
Intranet

questions.question: What should be done in Post Production phase?

options.text: Tuning and Hot fixing
Stabilization
User Guide Document
Testing

questions.question: What is important to understand, when disclosing concepts for an architecture design?

options.text: End user processes, business concepts, integration needs and NFRs
The major project risks
The availability of different teams involved in systems to integrate
Detailed user stories and signatures of all the required APIs

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a benefit of having well-defined application architecture?

options.text: Supports planning
Reduces risks
Reduces costs
Poor service abstraction

questions.question: Which role best describes the following: "Is selected from the Key Users to ensure representation and consensus of the end-users, being the go to person for question and problems for their area of expertise?"

options.text: Developer
Business Sponsor
Subject Matter Expert
Tech Lead

questions.question: How can you fix an upward reference from a library?

options.text: Always move the consumed elements from the producer to the consumer to avoid the reference
Simply remove the references to the elements of the upper layer module
Make sure the library is business agnostic and move consumed elements to reusable libraries
Identify the consumed elements and move them to a reusable core module

questions.question: When deploying applications with dependencies from Lifetime, which of the options is NOT true?

options.text: Red highlights inform if dependencies do not exist or are not updated in the target environment.
It is not necessary to solve any errors before proceeding because Lifetime has an automatic troubleshooting service.
The application version in the plan is also important because if the producers' versions are different in the destination, it is necessary to send the updated applications as well.
It is possible to open an application's dependency information and check what the error is.

questions.question: How can you fix an upward reference from a library?

options.text: Identify the consumed elements and move them to a reusable core module
Always move the consumed elements from the producer to the consumer to avoid the reference
Simply remove the references to the elements of the upper layer module
Make sure the library is business agnostic and move consumed elements to reusable libraries

questions.question: In which of the following scenarios should you choose to clone a built-in Style Guide?

options.text: It is not possible to customize a built-in Style Guide, since it is part of OutSystems UI
When minor customizations to the base theme should be done inside the app theme.
When it is not possible to benefit from any existing theme and extensive changes are needed.
When you want to introduce extensive changes to an existing theme.

questions.question: What is NOT expected from a Tech Lead during iterations?

options.text: Ensure solution architecture compliance and code maintainability principles
Lead development team and keep it focused on delivering on time, with quality, while following best practices
Together with the PO, protect the development team from disruptions
Negotiate with BA and remove user stories if there are time/resource constraints

questions.question: When applications have performance problems, what should be the starting point of tuning efforts:

options.text: Use Service Center logs and reports to identify the slowest operations.
Use Service Studio analysis to identify performance bottlenecks.
Add application logs to measure time spent in each relevant part of business logic.
Debug your applications and execute step-by-step business logic execution.

questions.question: Developer is expected to have a Developer mindset, which are NOT part of the Developer mindset?

options.text: Delivery - Develop the technical solution following best practices
Solution Architect - Analyze, integrate and design
Be the User - Impersonate the user to focus on business value
UI/UX - Ensure user friendly design to give maximum value

questions.question: Inside the service center in the Monitoring tab how old can be the logs presented?

options.text: Two weeks or less
Nine weeks or less
Three weeks or less

questions.question: What are the goals of the solution delivery?

options.text: In the initiation phase writing extensive documentation then following that plan in order to deliver the product
Time to market, Response to change, Value to end user

questions.question: Which of the below is NOT a reason why Business Vision workshop is done in person?

options.text: All of the above is a reason workshop is done in person
Due to short timeframe of 2 weeks
Key stakeholders, the business, and the users
Understand the journey and the environment

questions.question: What is NOT expected from a Business Analyst (During Iteration)?

options.text: Collaborate with team to prepare iteration
Protect the development team and commit to Iteration
Help development team on clarifications and feedback to ensure development is aligned with business needs
Groom and prioritise user stories

questions.question: Which role best describes the following: "Responsible to for the project. Manages and mitigates risks. Manages timeline. Coordinates budget and resources"?

options.text: Product Owner
Testers/ QA Coordinator
Project Manager

questions.question: Which method is Plan driven over Value Driven?

options.text: Waterfall
Kanban
Agile
Extreme GoHorse

questions.question: What is NOT expected from a Tech Lead before iteration?

options.text: Negotiate with End Users on requirements and prioritize user-stories
Design scalable and high performance architectures
Drill-down of requirements into well-defined tasks
Responsible for technical solution and non-functional requirements

questions.question: When deploying an application... what permissions should the user have have?

options.text: Change and deploy on source
Change and deploy on target

questions.question: What is NOT the responsibility of the Tech Lead?

options.text: Best practices (Lead development team and keep it focused on delivering on time, with quality, while following best practices)
Protect dev team from disruptions (alongside PO)
Be the user
Technical solution

questions.question: What are the 3 cycles that occur within of an Iteration Development:

options.text: Initiation, Shape and Build
Initiation, Build and Release
Shape, Build and Accept
Build, Accept and Release

questions.question: How to improve an External Core Service with local cache, when the whole database is too big and costly to synchronize and you need to list and search? (Details are only required for single entries)

options.text: Make sure the synchronization logic is separate from the core service to reduce impacts
Sync only summary data that it's frequently listed, joined or searched and get full detail directly from the external systems when a single entry is selected
Adopt a lazy load strategy, where the local cache is incrementally updated, whenever single records are requested from the external system
Avoid the local cache to remove the synchronization cost without performance impacts

questions.question: What is the the goal of a correct application composition?

options.text: Aggregate modules of same functional area, for different sponsors
Allow different paces in modules of the same Application
Minimize impacts between modules
Simplify deployments

questions.question: Adoption of the 3-layer canvas provides:

options.text: An automatic way to follow and fix architecture principles
A systematic approach to architecture design, supported by a validation tool
Faster architecture design without validation need
A common understanding between business users

questions.question: Which of the below is NOT part of Business Vision Workshop?

options.text: Around a strategic business concept ...
A short 2 week workshop that happens ...
Using UI/UX promotion ...
Before an Agile Project ...

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT TRUE about the result of the wrong application composition?

options.text: Applications that impact each other unnecessarily.
None of the above.
Applications not being able to evolve independently.
Deploying one application may result in having to take all other applications with it to Production.

questions.question: Which of the below is NOT part of the deliverables of the Business Vision?

options.text: Architecture Design - Describes technical foundation guidelines
Kick off Presentation - Describes Goals and expectations, Teams availability, Activities alignment and Deliverables
UX & UI Concept - Describes information architecture and user's journey
Vision Document - Describes Business Context, Drivers, User Stories, and top processes

questions.question: What Not to do during the Initiation Workshop?

options.text: Design UI Mock-ups
Define team members
Define the solution architecture
Write User Stories

questions.question: Which of the following options describes a valid reason to create a Calculation Engine (_Eng) module?

options.text: To support complex calculations (e.g. an insurance simulator).
To support reusable Actions from Core Widget modules.
To support a concept with entities and CRUD edition.
To abstract different complex logic implemented in several Business Logic modules.

questions.question: A Business Vision is similar to Project Initiation. What is the difference between a business Vision?

options.text: Project Initiation focus on the details, Business Vision focus on the high-level needs
Project Initiation is Project Oriented, Business Vision is Business Oriented
Project Initiation is about preparing the iteration, and what needs to be done, and this starts rightaway, Business Vision is about focusing on the business goals, and this is a long term focus
All of the above are differences

questions.question: Consider the following scenario: You have multiple applications with the same look and feel as an existing built-in style guide. All applications reuse the same federated authentication.

options.text: The Style guide is an specialization of the built-in and the Login flow is in the template
The Style guide is a clone of the built-in and the login flow is in the template
The Style guide is an specialization of the built-in and the Login flow is in the theme
The Style guide is a clone of the built-in and the Menu is in the theme
